records.ety: [F. encaisser; pref. en- (L. in) + caisse case. See Case a box, and cf. Encase, Enchase.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Incased (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Incasing.]

defs: To inclose in a case; to inclose; to cover or surround with something solid.
quotes: Rich plates of gold the folding doors incase.
